instruction: Show Rust configuration patterns using the config crate with serde deserialization, environment overrides, and typed config structs.
output:
<think>
Rust configuration follows similar layering to other languages but with strong typing via serde. The config crate is the standard solution. It supports: TOML/YAML/JSON files, environment variables, programmatic overrides, and hierarchical configs.

Key patterns:
1. Define config as strongly-typed structs with serde::Deserialize
2. Layer sources: defaults -> file -> env vars -> CLI
3. Environment variable prefix to avoid collisions
4. Nested structs map to nested config sections
5. Validate at startup, fail fast

The config crate merges all sources into a unified Value tree, then deserializes into your struct. Serde handles type conversion.
</think>

```rust
use serde::Deserialize;
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::time::Duration;

// Strongly-typed config structs
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct AppConfig {
    pub app_name: String,
    #[serde(default = "default_env")]
    pub environment: Environment,
    pub server: ServerConfig,
    pub database: DatabaseConfig,
    pub redis: Option<RedisConfig>,
    pub logging: LogConfig,
}

fn default_env() -> Environment { Environment::Development }

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone, PartialEq)]
#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
pub enum Environment {
    Development,
    Staging,
    Production,
}

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct ServerConfig {
    #[serde(default = "default_host")]
    pub host: String,
    #[serde(default = "default_port")]
    pub port: u16,
    #[serde(default = "default_workers")]
    pub workers: usize,
    #[serde(with = "humantime_serde", default = "default_timeout")]
    pub request_timeout: Duration,
}

fn default_host() -> String { "0.0.0.0".into() }
fn default_port() -> u16 { 8080 }
fn default_workers() -> usize { 4 }
fn default_timeout() -> Duration { Duration::from_secs(30) }

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct DatabaseConfig {
    pub url: String,
    #[serde(default = "default_pool")]
    pub pool_min: u32,
    #[serde(default = "default_pool_max")]
    pub pool_max: u32,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub ssl: bool,
}

fn default_pool() -> u32 { 5 }
fn default_pool_max() -> u32 { 20 }

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct RedisConfig {
    pub url: String,
    #[serde(default = "default_ttl")]
    pub ttl_secs: u64,
}

fn default_ttl() -> u64 { 3600 }

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct LogConfig {
    #[serde(default = "default_log_level")]
    pub level: String,
    pub file: Option<PathBuf>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub json: bool,
}

fn default_log_level() -> String { "info".into() }

impl AppConfig {
    /// Load config from file + environment variables.
    /// Priority: env vars > config file > defaults.
    pub fn load(config_path: Option<&str>) -> Result<Self, config::ConfigError> {
        let mut builder = config::Config::builder();

        // Layer 1: Config file (if exists)
        if let Some(path) = config_path {
            builder = builder.add_source(
                config::File::with_name(path).required(false)
            );
        }

        // Layer 2: Environment variables (APP_SERVER__PORT=9090)
        builder = builder.add_source(
            config::Environment::with_prefix("APP")
                .separator("__")
                .try_parsing(true)
        );

        let config = builder.build()?;
        config.try_deserialize()
    }

    /// Validate config after loading.
    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), Vec<String>> {
        let mut errors = Vec::new();

        if self.server.port == 0 {
            errors.push("server.port cannot be 0".into());
        }
        if self.server.workers == 0 {
            errors.push("server.workers must be positive".into());
        }
        if self.database.pool_max < self.database.pool_min {
            errors.push("database.pool_max must be >= pool_min".into());
        }
        if self.environment == Environment::Production {
            if self.server.workers < 2 {
                errors.push("production needs at least 2 workers".into());
            }
            if self.database.url.contains("localhost") {
                errors.push("production should not use localhost DB".into());
            }
        }

        if errors.is_empty() { Ok(()) } else { Err(errors) }
    }
}

// config.toml:
// app_name = "my-service"
// environment = "production"
//
// [server]
// host = "0.0.0.0"
// port = 8080
// workers = 8
// request_timeout = "30s"
//
// [database]
// url = "postgres://user:pass@db:5432/mydb"
// pool_max = 20
// ssl = true
//
// [logging]
// level = "warn"
// json = true

fn main() {
    let config = AppConfig::load(Some("config"))
        .expect("Failed to load config");

    if let Err(errors) = config.validate() {
        eprintln!("Config validation failed:");
        for e in &errors {
            eprintln!("  - {e}");
        }
        std::process::exit(1);
    }

    println!("Loaded: {:?}", config);
}
```

instruction: Show how to handle configuration with feature flags in Rust — compile-time config vs runtime config, and typed builder patterns.
output:
<think>
Rust offers two distinct configuration approaches:

1. Compile-time: Feature flags, cfg attributes, const/static values. Zero runtime cost but requires recompilation.
2. Runtime: Config files, env vars, CLI args. Flexible but has overhead.

The builder pattern for config is popular in Rust because:
- Required vs optional fields are explicit
- Validation happens at build time
- Default values are clear
- Type state can enforce required fields

I'll show both compile-time config with cfg and a typed builder for runtime config.
</think>

```rust
use std::net::SocketAddr;
use std::time::Duration;

// === Compile-time config with cfg ===

#[cfg(feature = "production")]
const DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL: &str = "warn";
#[cfg(not(feature = "production"))]
const DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL: &str = "debug";

#[cfg(feature = "production")]
const DEFAULT_WORKERS: usize = 8;
#[cfg(not(feature = "production"))]
const DEFAULT_WORKERS: usize = 1;

// Feature-gated module
#[cfg(feature = "metrics")]
mod metrics {
    pub fn init_prometheus(port: u16) {
        println!("Metrics server on :{port}");
    }
}

// === Runtime config with typed builder ===

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct ServerConfig {
    pub bind_addr: SocketAddr,
    pub workers: usize,
    pub request_timeout: Duration,
    pub max_body_size: usize,
    pub tls_cert: Option<String>,
    pub tls_key: Option<String>,
    pub log_level: String,
}

#[derive(Default)]
pub struct ServerConfigBuilder {
    bind_addr: Option<SocketAddr>,
    workers: Option<usize>,
    request_timeout: Option<Duration>,
    max_body_size: Option<usize>,
    tls_cert: Option<String>,
    tls_key: Option<String>,
    log_level: Option<String>,
}

impl ServerConfigBuilder {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self::default()
    }

    pub fn bind_addr(mut self, addr: SocketAddr) -> Self {
        self.bind_addr = Some(addr);
        self
    }

    pub fn workers(mut self, n: usize) -> Self {
        self.workers = Some(n);
        self
    }

    pub fn request_timeout(mut self, d: Duration) -> Self {
        self.request_timeout = Some(d);
        self
    }

    pub fn max_body_size(mut self, bytes: usize) -> Self {
        self.max_body_size = Some(bytes);
        self
    }

    pub fn tls(mut self, cert: impl Into<String>, key: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.tls_cert = Some(cert.into());
        self.tls_key = Some(key.into());
        self
    }

    pub fn log_level(mut self, level: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.log_level = Some(level.into());
        self
    }

    pub fn build(self) -> Result<ServerConfig, String> {
        let bind_addr = self.bind_addr
            .ok_or("bind_addr is required")?;

        let workers = self.workers.unwrap_or(DEFAULT_WORKERS);
        if workers == 0 {
            return Err("workers must be > 0".into());
        }

        let timeout = self.request_timeout
            .unwrap_or(Duration::from_secs(30));

        // TLS: both or neither
        match (&self.tls_cert, &self.tls_key) {
            (Some(_), None) | (None, Some(_)) => {
                return Err("Both tls_cert and tls_key required".into());
            }
            _ => {}
        }

        Ok(ServerConfig {
            bind_addr,
            workers,
            request_timeout: timeout,
            max_body_size: self.max_body_size.unwrap_or(10 * 1024 * 1024),
            tls_cert: self.tls_cert,
            tls_key: self.tls_key,
            log_level: self.log_level.unwrap_or_else(|| DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL.into()),
        })
    }
}

// Convenience: load from env into builder
impl ServerConfigBuilder {
    pub fn from_env(mut self) -> Self {
        if let Ok(addr) = std::env::var("BIND_ADDR") {
            if let Ok(parsed) = addr.parse() {
                self.bind_addr = Some(parsed);
            }
        }
        if let Ok(w) = std::env::var("WORKERS") {
            if let Ok(n) = w.parse() {
                self.workers = Some(n);
            }
        }
        if let Ok(level) = std::env::var("LOG_LEVEL") {
            self.log_level = Some(level);
        }
        self
    }
}

fn main() -> Result<(), String> {
    let config = ServerConfigBuilder::new()
        .from_env()  // Load env vars first
        .bind_addr("0.0.0.0:8080".parse().unwrap())  // Programmatic override
        .workers(4)
        .request_timeout(Duration::from_secs(60))
        .build()?;

    println!("Config: {config:?}");

    #[cfg(feature = "metrics")]
    metrics::init_prometheus(9090);

    Ok(())
}
```

instruction: Show how to deserialize complex Rust config with serde including custom deserializers, flattened fields, and environment-aware defaults.
output:
<think>
Advanced serde patterns for configuration:

1. Custom deserializers for special types (Duration from string, bytes from human-readable)
2. Flatten for composing config sections
3. Default trait implementation for environment-aware defaults
4. Rename/alias for flexible field names
5. Deny unknown fields for strict validation

These patterns make config files user-friendly while keeping the Rust types strict.
</think>

```rust
use serde::Deserialize;
use serde::de::{self, Deserializer};
use std::time::Duration;
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::fmt;

// Custom duration deserializer: "30s", "5m", "1h"
fn deserialize_duration<'de, D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Duration, D::Error>
where
    D: Deserializer<'de>,
{
    let s = String::deserialize(deserializer)?;
    parse_duration(&s).map_err(de::Error::custom)
}

fn parse_duration(s: &str) -> Result<Duration, String> {
    let s = s.trim();
    if let Some(n) = s.strip_suffix('s') {
        Ok(Duration::from_secs(n.trim().parse::<u64>().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?))
    } else if let Some(n) = s.strip_suffix('m') {
        Ok(Duration::from_secs(n.trim().parse::<u64>().map_err(|e| e.to_string())? * 60))
    } else if let Some(n) = s.strip_suffix('h') {
        Ok(Duration::from_secs(n.trim().parse::<u64>().map_err(|e| e.to_string())? * 3600))
    } else {
        Ok(Duration::from_secs(s.parse::<u64>().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?))
    }
}

// Human-readable byte sizes: "10MB", "1GB"
fn deserialize_bytes<'de, D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<usize, D::Error>
where
    D: Deserializer<'de>,
{
    let s = String::deserialize(deserializer)?;
    parse_bytes(&s).map_err(de::Error::custom)
}

fn parse_bytes(s: &str) -> Result<usize, String> {
    let s = s.trim().to_uppercase();
    if let Some(n) = s.strip_suffix("GB") {
        Ok(n.trim().parse::<usize>().map_err(|e| e.to_string())? * 1024 * 1024 * 1024)
    } else if let Some(n) = s.strip_suffix("MB") {
        Ok(n.trim().parse::<usize>().map_err(|e| e.to_string())? * 1024 * 1024)
    } else if let Some(n) = s.strip_suffix("KB") {
        Ok(n.trim().parse::<usize>().map_err(|e| e.to_string())? * 1024)
    } else {
        s.parse::<usize>().map_err(|e| e.to_string())
    }
}

// Flattened TLS config
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct TlsConfig {
    pub cert_path: PathBuf,
    pub key_path: PathBuf,
    #[serde(default = "default_tls_versions")]
    pub min_version: String,
}

fn default_tls_versions() -> String { "1.2".into() }

// Main config with all patterns
#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]
#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]  // Catch typos in config
pub struct ServiceConfig {
    #[serde(alias = "name", alias = "service_name")]
    pub app_name: String,

    #[serde(default = "default_bind")]
    pub bind: String,

    #[serde(deserialize_with = "deserialize_duration", default = "default_timeout")]
    pub timeout: Duration,

    #[serde(deserialize_with = "deserialize_bytes", default = "default_body_limit")]
    pub max_body_size: usize,

    // Optional TLS — flatten puts tls_cert_path, tls_key_path at top level
    pub tls: Option<TlsConfig>,

    // Nested configs
    pub database: DatabaseConfig,

    #[serde(default)]
    pub features: FeatureFlags,
}

fn default_bind() -> String { "0.0.0.0:8080".into() }
fn default_timeout() -> Duration { Duration::from_secs(30) }
fn default_body_limit() -> usize { 10 * 1024 * 1024 }

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone)]
pub struct DatabaseConfig {
    pub url: String,
    #[serde(deserialize_with = "deserialize_duration", default = "default_db_timeout")]
    pub connect_timeout: Duration,
    #[serde(default = "default_pool_size")]
    pub pool_size: u32,
}

fn default_db_timeout() -> Duration { Duration::from_secs(5) }
fn default_pool_size() -> u32 { 10 }

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Clone, Default)]
pub struct FeatureFlags {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enable_cache: bool,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enable_metrics: bool,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enable_rate_limiting: bool,
    #[serde(default = "default_rate_limit")]
    pub rate_limit_rps: u32,
}

fn default_rate_limit() -> u32 { 100 }

// TOML config file:
// app_name = "my-service"
// bind = "0.0.0.0:9090"
// timeout = "60s"
// max_body_size = "50MB"
//
// [database]
// url = "postgres://user:pass@localhost/db"
// connect_timeout = "10s"
// pool_size = 20
//
// [tls]
// cert_path = "/etc/ssl/cert.pem"
// key_path = "/etc/ssl/key.pem"
//
// [features]
// enable_cache = true
// enable_metrics = true
// rate_limit_rps = 500

fn main() {
    let toml_str = r#"
        app_name = "demo"
        timeout = "45s"
        max_body_size = "25MB"

        [database]
        url = "postgres://localhost/test"
    "#;

    let config: ServiceConfig = toml::from_str(toml_str).unwrap();
    println!("Timeout: {:?}", config.timeout);
    println!("Body limit: {} bytes", config.max_body_size);
    println!("Features: {:?}", config.features);
}
```
